prompt:
We have a small in-house Java client for SDDC Manager (Json/SddcHttpClient/DomainsClient in this repo) that our inventory jobs use, and today it's driven by an access token somebody pastes into an env var. VCF access tokens are short-lived, so the jobs die mid-run every hour and — worse — when someone turned on debug logging last sprint, the bearer token showed up in the job log. I want a real authentication layer added.

Research first: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official SDDC Manager Tokens API pages on developer.broadcom.com — create token pair, refresh access token, invalidate refresh token — plus the domains list page our read path uses. Watch the details: the refresh call's body and content type are NOT what you'd guess from the old 5.2 docs, and the create response status changed too. Our pinned wire contract is in docs/contract.json, provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both protected. If live docs disagree with the fixtures, say so in your summary and leave them alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

The feature — a new TokenManager class (plus whatever helpers you want) that implements VcfTransport and AutoCloseable so DomainsClient can ride on it unchanged:

- TokenManager.connect(baseUrl, username, password, httpClient, logSink) logs in via the documented create-token-pair operation and keeps the pair. logSink is a Consumer<String> we wire to our job logger.
- get(path) attaches the bearer token. On a 401 it refreshes ONCE using the documented refresh operation and retries the original request once; a second 401 is VcfAuthException. A 403 is emphatically NOT a refresh trigger — role problems shouldn't generate token churn.
- Several worker threads share one manager. If they all trip over the same expired token at once I want ONE refresh on the wire, not four — serialize it; latecomers reuse the rotated token.
- close() invalidates the refresh token via the documented operation (once — closing twice must not fire twice), after which the manager refuses to work.
- Nothing secret ever reaches logSink or an exception message: not the password, not access tokens, not the refresh token id. But the log must still show that refreshes happen, or ops can't debug token churn.

Existing behavior must keep working: DomainsClient over the plain SddcHttpClient with a static token is used by two other jobs — don't break it.

TestMain.java is the acceptance contract (loopback fake SDDC Manager, dummy credentials only). Run java TestMain.java until it prints all checks passed. TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager (Tokens API)
research.interface: SDDC Manager REST API 9.1 (developer.broadcom.com xAPIs, /v1 base path)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Create Token Pair (POST /v1/tokens)
official_sources.used_for: TokenCreationSpec {username, password, apiKey, idToken}; 201 Created returning TokenPair {accessToken, refreshToken:{id}}; 400/500 Error responses; access token is a bearer token for public API calls

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Refresh Access Token (PATCH /v1/tokens/access-token/refresh)
official_sources.used_for: request body is the refresh token id serialized as a bare JSON string with Content-Type application/json; 200 returns the new access token as a JSON string; 400/404/500 Error responses

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Invalidate Refresh Token (DELETE /v1/tokens/refresh-token)
official_sources.used_for: request body is the refresh token id as a JSON string with Content-Type application/json; 204 No Content on success

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Retrieve a list of domains (GET /v1/domains)
official_sources.used_for: PageOfDomain {elements:[Domain], pageMetadata} and Domain fields (id, name, type, status, ...) used by the existing read path the token layer must keep working

official_sources.title: VMware Cloud Foundation API 5.2 - Tokens APIs
official_sources.used_for: version-discipline context: the 5.2 docs show the refresh operation with a text/plain body and a 200 create response, while the current 9.1 pages document application/json and 201 Created - the client targets the current 9.1 contract

research.language_rationale: Broadcom publishes no official Java SDK for the SDDC Manager API; the documented surface is REST over /v1, and java.net.http.HttpClient from the JDK is the idiomatic zero-dependency transport for a shared authentication layer used by several in-house Java services.
